purpose: Define a visual-truth proof packet: the durable, independently re-checkable justification for one read-only visual or color measurement. It joins an artifact digest, declared color assumptions, embedded sRGB sample values, measurements recomputed from those samples, honest display caveats, an explicit calibration boundary, and a derived MATCH, DRIFT, or UNVERIFIABLE verdict. The packet never mutates hardware: it records and re-checks measurements, it never writes a monitor, LUT, or ICC profile, and it never claims a physical calibration it did not perform.

sources_checked.name: project-telos.proof-packet/v1
sources_checked.role: supplies the sibling agent-action lane style this contract mirrors: canonical hash scope, derived-not-asserted verdict, recompute-from-materials verifier, and the frozen conformance-fixture and negative-test-case blocks

sources_checked.name: visual-measurement-proof-packet/v0
sources_checked.role: the external export target the exporter conforms to; validated read-only with proof-surface on sys.path, never imported or spawned at runtime

contract.verification_required: Every packet verdict is MATCH, DRIFT, or UNVERIFIABLE. PROMOTED and any physical-calibration claim are never assertable from a single read-only packet.

measurement_recompute_relation.claim: A measurement is a load-bearing claim, not an assertion. Every measurement value is recomputed from the artifact's embedded sRGB sample values using stdlib math only. A measurement whose method points at samples the artifact does not carry cannot be recomputed: that is an UNVERIFIABLE evidence gap named by its JSON path, never a silent pass.

recompute_methods.relative_luminance_srgb: Linearize each embedded sRGB channel with the IEC 61966-2-1 transfer, then take the Rec. 709 weighted sum 0.2126 R + 0.7152 G + 0.0722 B. The sample is named by artifact.samples[<id>].
recompute_methods.delta_e_cie76: Convert two embedded sRGB samples to CIE Lab under the D65 white point, then take the Euclidean distance between the two Lab points. The two samples are named by measurement.sample_refs.

measurement_recompute_relation.tolerance_rule: deviation is the absolute difference between the claimed value and the recomputed value. A deviation at or below tolerance is a per-metric MATCH; a deviation above tolerance is a per-metric DRIFT carrying the recomputed value; a measurement with no recomputable basis is a per-metric UNVERIFIABLE.

assembler.accepts: a conformance fixture (happy_path or an operator-supplied fixture)
assembler.returns: a canonical visual proof packet plus packet_hash over the hash scope

verifier.returns: named check results, a typed failure list, and a derived verdict MATCH, DRIFT, or UNVERIFIABLE

export_contract.decision_summary_source: derived from the packet overall verdict, not from any embedded decision field

decision_summary_map.note: proof-surface overall verdict to decision_summary, from _decision.derive_decision_summary

failure_label_map.note: telos visual failure codes to proof-surface FAILURE_CODES (closed set in _failure.py at proof-surface HEAD)

happy_path.claim: The mid-gray patch renders at the recomputed relative luminance and the two warm-red swatches differ by the recomputed CIE76 delta-E, both within their declared tolerances, from the artifact's embedded sRGB samples and with no display touched.
happy_path.scope: one read-only sRGB color measurement over an embedded swatch strip in the Telos visual-proof-lane demo
display_caveats: values are computed from the artifact's embedded sRGB samples, not measured off a physical display
no monitor, LUT, or ICC profile was read or written; the packet is read-only by construction
relative luminance assumes the sRGB IEC 61966-2-1 transfer and the Rec. 709 luminance weights
delta-E is CIE76 under the D65 white point and is a first-order color-difference estimate, not a perceptual guarantee

calibration_boundary.note: no colorimeter or spectrophotometer was used; no display state was mutated; this packet measures encoded sample values only

tampered_measurement_value.mutate: replace measurements[0] value with a wrong number outside tolerance while the embedded samples stay unchanged

measurement_without_recomputable_basis.mutate: point measurements[0] sample_refs at a sample id the artifact does not carry

physical_calibration_overclaim.mutate: set calibration_boundary.physical_calibration_claim true while the packet stays read-only with no hardware measurement
